fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Iowa Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Iowa - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity iowa, how to transfer physical therapist license to iowa, physical therapist compact iowa, iowa physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Iowa Board of Physical and Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: Department of Inspections, Appeals, and Licensing (DIAL)
board.mailing_address: Iowa Board of Physical & Occupational Therapy, 321 E 12th St, Des Moines, IA 50319-0075

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Iowa.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Access the HHS Online Services portal (https://hpl.hhs.iowa.gov/), register for a new account, and then complete and submit the 'Initial Application' for a Physical Therapist license. Pay the application fee of $120.00.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying your professional degree, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Iowa Board of Physical & Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Pass the NPTE (Initial Licensure by Examination)
steps.description: Register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) and pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE). Ensure scores are sent directly from FSBPT to the Iowa Board. (This step may be waived for endorsement applicants who meet specific practice experience criteria).

steps.title: Complete the Iowa Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the online Iowa Jurisprudence Examination, which assesses your knowledge of Iowa's laws and rules pertaining to physical therapy practice.

steps.title: Complete a Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Iowa Board of Physical & Occupational Therapy on their website to complete the required criminal background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Iowa physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $120. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Iowa have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Iowa participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Iowa without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Iowa?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Iowa
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Iowa, you must submit an initial application through the HHS Online Services portal and fulfill all required documentation and examination criteria.

variants.label: New Applicant

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to https://hpl.hhs.iowa.gov/, click 'Register a New Account' to create your profile, then log in and select 'Initial Application' for Physical Therapist.
